companies.timeline_note: 103 reviews span roughly 2019–2025. A sharp negative IT review (Nov 2025) coexists with strong 2024–2025 site ratings — older negatives may not reflect current culture if the company changed since 2023.
companies.trend_summary: 2024–2025 reviews praise benefits, career growth, and project exposure; recurring negatives are tight deadlines and work-life balance. Sep 2021 and Mar 2020 flag poor WLB and internal politics. Nov 2025 IT review is a recent outlier on leadership culture.

sample_reviews.positive: Remuneration is fair.
sample_reviews.negative: Lazy leadership style management and culture; IT strategy misaligned with civil-engineering core.

sample_reviews.positive: Great career growth, competitive benefits, positive work culture, innovative global projects.
sample_reviews.negative: High expectations, tight deadlines, fast-paced environment.

sample_reviews.positive: Happy working 6 years; good benefits and working environment.
sample_reviews.negative: Many things to learn — steep learning curve.

sample_reviews.positive: Good exposure, monthly trainings, Toastmasters, good benefits for fresh graduates.
sample_reviews.negative: Extremely poor work-life balance; high expectations for juniors.

sample_reviews.positive: Organisation promotes positive philosophy on paper.
sample_reviews.negative: Appraisal bias; philosophy described as fake; only ~20% understand it.

companies.ai_summary: Employees appreciate friendly colleagues, teamwork culture, good benefits and fair remuneration. Valuable learning opportunities and exposure to innovative construction projects. Challenges include work-life balance due to tight deadlines, varying management styles, demanding workloads, and frequent travel between offices and sites.

companies.timeline_note: 25 reviews mostly from 2020–2024. Weight reviews from the last 18 months higher when assessing current working culture.
companies.trend_summary: Consistently positive on team support, training for fresh graduates, and benefits. Peak-period workload and Mandarin workplace dynamics appear in older reviews; no strong deterioration signal in recent samples.

sample_reviews.positive: Supportive team, open-door management, good training.
sample_reviews.negative: Heavy workload during closing periods.

sample_reviews.positive: Collaborative cross-discipline learning, timely pay.
sample_reviews.negative: Limited parking and dining nearby.

sample_reviews.positive: Friendly colleagues, competitive benefits.
sample_reviews.negative: Politics in some departments.

companies.ai_summary: Supportive team environment with friendly colleagues and strong learning opportunities, especially for fresh graduates. Competitive benefits, open-door management, and positive culture. Challenges include peak-period workloads, structured approval tiers, and Mandarin-speaking workplace dynamics for some roles.

companies.timeline_note: No curated local reviews yet — live benchmark will scrape Jobstreet on verification.
companies.ai_summary: Jobstreet profile configured for live benchmark retrieval. Use OpenRouter to scrape latest employee ratings and working-environment themes.

companies.timeline_note: 89 reviews span ~2018–2025. Site-role reviews from 2022–2024 flag WLB issues; compare against any 2025 sustainability claims about employee wellbeing.
companies.trend_summary: Mega-project exposure and friendly colleagues remain stable positives. Management communication and career pathways are recurring negatives across years; recent reviews still mention demanding site schedules.

sample_reviews.positive: Mega-project exposure, friendly colleagues, timely salary.
sample_reviews.negative: Poor work-life balance on some sites.

sample_reviews.positive: Good teamwork across construction and property divisions.
sample_reviews.negative: Upper-management transparency gaps.

sample_reviews.positive: Learning opportunities on large projects.
sample_reviews.negative: Limited career development pathways.

companies.ai_summary: Friendly supportive colleagues and good teamwork with exposure to mega projects across construction, property, hospitality and aviation. Timely salary and learning opportunities. Challenges include upper-management transparency, demanding work-life balance on some projects, and room to improve systems and career pathways.
sample_themes_negative: Poor work-life balance on some sites

companies.timeline_note: 71 reviews span ~2017–2024. Manufacturing/operations roles report heavier hours — cross-check against 2025 social claims on training and wellbeing.
companies.trend_summary: Family-like culture and training programmes praised consistently since 2019. Operations roles flag demanding schedules; some units note old-school management and uneven progression clarity.

sample_reviews.positive: Supportive environment, comprehensive training, good benefits.
sample_reviews.negative: Demanding schedules in operations.

sample_reviews.positive: Strong training programmes, trust from management.
sample_reviews.negative: Unclear development pathways in some units.

sample_reviews.positive: Family-like supportive culture.
sample_reviews.negative: Traditional management approaches in places.

companies.ai_summary: Supportive work environment with friendly colleagues and comprehensive training. Attractive benefits and trust from management with career progression potential. Challenges include demanding schedules in manufacturing/operations, traditional management approaches, and unclear development pathways in some units.

companies.timeline_note: 56 reviews mostly 2019–2024. Appraisal politics mentioned in 2021–2023 reviews — verify whether 2025 culture initiatives changed this.
companies.trend_summary: Benefits and mentor quality stay strong across samples. Tight deadlines and review-period politics recur; work-life balance scores remain below overall rating.

sample_reviews.positive: Strong medical coverage and bonuses, experienced mentors.
sample_reviews.negative: Tight project deadlines.

sample_reviews.positive: Diverse friendly team, good learning.
sample_reviews.negative: Internal politics during appraisals.

sample_reviews.positive: Excellent learning with experienced colleagues.
sample_reviews.negative: Work pressure and long hours.

companies.ai_summary: Strong employee benefits including medical coverage and bonuses. Excellent learning with experienced colleagues in a friendly diverse environment. Challenges include tight deadlines, internal politics during appraisals, variable management styles, and demanding schedules affecting work-life balance.

companies.timeline_note: Only 7 reviews, oldest from Jul 2015 and newest Sep 2023 — no post-2023 reviews. A harsh Sep 2023 site-supervisor review may not reflect 2025+ changes claimed in sustainability reports; treat pre-2020 positives as historical context.
companies.trend_summary: 2015–2017 reviews praise kind leadership, teamwork, and a beginner-friendly site culture. 2019–2020 still positive on colleagues but flag public-holiday work and weak WLB. Sep 2023 is sharply negative on benefits and self-reliance — possible deterioration or isolated role, but no recent reviews confirm improvement.

sample_reviews.positive: None cited.
sample_reviews.negative: No standards or good benefits; heavy workload; must survive alone with no support.

sample_reviews.positive: Hope to work together.
sample_reviews.negative: Not accepted here — no further comment.

sample_reviews.positive: Friendly helpful colleagues willing to teach.
sample_reviews.negative: Public holidays still require work; poor work-life balance.

sample_reviews.positive: Great boss; all perks.
sample_reviews.negative: Adapting to new technologies.

sample_reviews.positive: Kind motivating boss despite datuk title; humble and inspiring.
sample_reviews.negative: Six-day weeks; involved in everything from planning to construction.

companies.ai_summary: Employees appreciate friendly and helpful colleagues, a kind and motivating boss, and a work environment suited to beginners, with valued teamwork. Challenges include heavy workload with long hours and weekend work, weak work-life balance (including public-holiday duties), adapting to new technologies, and cultural or language barriers linked to a large Bangladeshi site workforce.
